description: Maze planning-cost comparison on a FROZEN world model (exp_value = exp_aux_pos weights + a TD-MPC value head trained on the model's own rollouts). Success rate = fraction of 16 held-out 21x21 mazes solved (eval_maze_med, n_allowed_steps=180). Same world model and same MPPI settings across costs; only the planning objective changes.
chance_note: no chance baseline for navigation; 0% = never reaches the goal
headline: At the tuned waypoint regime the learned TD-MPC value reaches 37.5% vs 6.25% for the geometric distance cost (6x), validating that a learned cost-to-go guides planning far better than raw representation/position distance. Greedy-global is 0% for every cost (goal beyond the value's trained horizon / latent distance dominated by walls).
